data.title: Credo Technology surges as Wall Street sees another AI beneficiary
data.description:
It looks like Credo Technology (NASDAQ:CRDO) is the next beneficiary from the artificial intelligence spending boom.

Credo, which makes connectivity solutions for optical and Ethernet applications, reported stronger-than-expected third-quarter results and guidance that led to much praise from Wall Street, including a rare double upgrade, as analysts see a "growth inflection" at the Bill Brennan-led company.

Shares rose 32% in premarket trading.

Bank of America analyst Vivek Arya upped his rating on Credo to Buy from Underperform and raised his price target to $80 from $27, as the company saw a "material" growth inflection in the second-half, based on AI cluster adoption.

"Near term, we see the company driving a strong 2HFY25E (Jan’25Q plus Apr’25Q) driven by strong AI growth at Amazon Web Services, a key customer (and investor) at CRDO," Arya wrote in a note to clients. "Our prior cautious view was based on limited [total addressable market] assumptions for [active electrical cables], but CRDO’s earnings call addressed those concerns nicely, with 3x 10% customers (Microsoft, Amazon, Tesla), and growing momentum in other adjacent areas (optical DSP, line card PHY, retimer)."

And while Arya still sees competition in active electrical cables space from companies like Marvell (MRVL) and Broadcom (AVGO), there is enough customer traction and AI product ramps to sustain the market, Arya added.

Stifel analyst Tore Svanberg reiterated his Buy rating and upped his price target to $75 from $50 after what he called a "strong beat and remarkable raise." Credo is benefiting from strong growth for active electrical cables, but continued trends in the data center market (like enhanced power sourcing and liquid cooling) have added to higher densification, opening up new areas for active electrical cables, Svanberg explained.

Needham analyst N. Quinn Bolton also upped his price target on Credo after the results (moving to $70 from $43) and said he has been waiting for the second-half inflection since January. Now that it's finally come, it's stronger-than-expected, due in part to a larger number of XPU deployments.

Credo should also see continued growth in the active electrical cables market as there should be a higher attach rate per XPU and the transition to higher speed active electrical cables should also benefit the company, Bolton added.

Lastly, Credo looks to be moving past the Amazon (AMZN) warrant headwind in the fiscal fourth-quarter, so gross margins should rise faster-than-expected, Bolton added.

data.title: Axon Enterprise upgraded at Morgan Stanley on AI growth outlook
data.description:
Axon Enterprise (NASDAQ:AXON) on Tuesday was upgraded to Overweight from a previous investment rating of Equal Weight by analysts at financial-services firm Morgan Stanley.

They said the maker of Taser devices and body cams for law enforcement is poised for continued growth as artificial intelligence technology becomes a bigger part of its sales mix and helps to upsell premium packages of products.

“We have increasing confidence that the approximately 25%-30% growth Axon (AXON) is seeing is durable as software continues to become a growing portion of the model,” Meta Marshall, analyst at Morgan Stanley, said in a December 3 report. “While the name is expensive and may need to grow into valuation given run year-to-date, over the long term, we see it as a core holding.”

Morgan Stanley raised its price target on Axon (AXON) to $700 a share from $500 a share, based on an estimated enterprise value that’s 16 times sales for 2026.

“Even at current valuation, we view risk reward attractively in our space and would be buyers of the name on weakness on any rotation pullbacks,” the bank said.

data.title: Cyber Monday estimates peg total sales at a record $13.2B to $13.5B
data.description:
Early estimates for Cyber Monday continue to show record-breaking sales, as Adobe Analytics is forecasting the total at $13.2B to $13.5B, making it the biggest online shopping day of all time.

Consumers will have seen the biggest deals on electronics, off 30% the regular price, followed by 23% discounts for apparel, 23% off on computers, 17% off on appliances, and 16% discounts on sporting goods.

With plentiful discounts, shoppers seemed less concerned about any lingering inflationary pressures. Adobe found that online spending continues to be driven by net-new-demand and not higher prices.

As was the case for Black Friday, mobile shopping was again the preferred method during Cyber Monday with 9.6% more transactions being conducted on mobile devices (54.8% of all online sales). Top items remained toys, jewelry, apparel, sporting goods, personal care items, electronics, and appliances.

Adobe analytics tickers: Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN), Mattel (NASDAQ:MAT), Hasbro (NASDAQ:HAS), Best Buy (NYSE:BBY), Walmart (NYSE:WMT), Target (NYSE:TGT), JAKKS Pacific (NASDAQ:JAKK), GameStop (NYSE:GME), Abercrombie & Fitch (NYSE:ANF), Gap (NYSE:GAP), Victoria’s Secret (NYSE:VSCO), Ross Stores (NASDAQ:ROST), TJX Companies (NYSE:TJX), Foot Locker (NYSE:FL), Deckers Outdoor (NYSE:DECK), DICK’s Sporting Goods (NYSE:DKS), Bath & Body Works (NYSE:BBWI), Ulta (NASDAQ:ULTA), Five Below (NASDAQ:FIVE), Burlington Stores (NYSE:BURL), Urban Outfitters (NASDAQ:URBN), SPDR S&P Retail ETF (NYSEARCA:XRT), Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLY).

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow futures tread cautiously with labor data on tap
data.description:
Stock index futures were almost flat on Tuesday, as investors turn their attention to a key labor report.

S&P 500 futures (SPX), Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND), and Dow futures (INDU) -0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.22%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat to 4.19%.

Major market averages closed out Monday’s session in a mixed fashion as the financial community digested the latest comments from President-elect Donald Trump as he threatened to implement more trade tariffs.

"Markets actually put in a decent performance yesterday, with the S&P 500 moving up to yet another record high. One supportive factor was an upside surprise in the ISM manufacturing for November," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

That equity rally was led by further strength among big tech stocks, and the Nasdaq and Magnificent 7 moved up to record highs of their own, Reid added.

The October Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey (JOLTS) report will land during market hours. It is one of the first of a few labor data expected in the week. Economists forecast a rise to 7.490M.

"The survey response rate is so low the numbers would not be published elsewhere. Recent variations in vacancies represented shifts between internal and external advertised positions," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Vacancies may be in focus if President-elect Donald Trump pursues deportations that create labor market bottlenecks, Donovan added.

data.title: BGC Group sells Capitalab for $46M to focus on Fenics
data.description:
BGC Group (NASDAQ:BGC) sold Capitalab, its rates portfolio compression and margin optimization business, to Capitolis, for $46M in gross proceeds, as it focuses on Fenics, its higher-margin, technology-driven business that generated more than $550M in revenue over the past year, the company said Tuesday.

The sale price represents a revenue multiple of ~7.5x. The one-time gain associated with the transaction will be included in BGC's consolidated GAAP results, but will be excluded from the company's adjusted earnings.

BGC will retain its high-growth, post-trade foreign exchange risk reduction business, which was previously included under the Capitalab brand and will be renamed Fenics NDF Match.

"The sale of Capitalab is yet another example of BGC unlocking value for its shareholders. We will continue to focus on growing our high-growth electronic offerings," said BGC Chief Operating Officer and Global Co-Head of Brokerage Sean Windeatt.

BGC Group (NASDAQ:BGC) stock fell 1.1% in premarket trading.

data.title: Constellation Brands to divest SVEDKA vodka brand
data.description:
Constellation Brands (NYSE:STZ) has reached an agreement with Sazerac to divest its SVEDKA vodka brand as it continues to reshape its wine and spirits portfolio towards the higher end.

This transaction is subject to customary closing conditions and expected to close in the coming months.

Over the last few years, Constellation has divested the majority of its popular and mainstream wine and spirits brands within the portfolio and is focused on competing predominantly in premium and fine wine and craft spirits segments.

“The actions we have taken over the past several years to reshape our wine and spirits portfolio support our efforts to accelerate the performance of that business,” said Bill Newlands, President and Chief Executive Officer, Constellation Brands. “This transaction is another step forward in seeking to ensure that our wine and spirits portfolio is optimized to succeed and to meet our growth objectives.”

data.title: Vestas wins 342 MW order for New York wind project
data.description:
Vestas (OTCPK:VWDRY) said Tuesday it was awarded a contract by U.S. clean energy developer Invenergy to deliver 342 MW of turbines for a 30K-acre wind farm project in New York.

The order is for Vestas' (OTCPK:VWDRY) V150-4.5 MW turbines, which will be installed at the Alle-Catt Wind Energy project, as well as a 10-year services agreement.

Vestas (OTCPK:VWDRY) said turbine delivery is planned for Q4 2025 and commissioning is anticipated in late 2026.

Invenergy obtained a siting permit for the $454M project in June.

data.title: Saia dips after reporting QTD LTL operating data
data.description:
• Saia (NASDAQ:SAIA) has announced October LTL shipments per workday increased 4.4%, LTL tonnage per workday increased 6.9% and LTL weight per shipment increased 2.4% to 1,352 pounds compared to 1,321 pounds in October 2023.
  • November LTL shipments per workday increased 2.3%, LTL tonnage per workday increased 5.7% and LTL weight per shipment increased 3.3% to 1,357 pounds compared to 1,313 pounds in November 2023.
  • SAIA -1% premarket to $552.0
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Kelly to effect share repurchase program
data.description:
• Specialty talent solutions provider Kelly (NASDAQ:KELYA) (NASDAQ:KELYB) said on Tuesday that its board has approved an up to $50M of class A share repurchase program.
  • The authorization expires on Dec. 2, 2026.
  • The shares may be purchased from time to time in the open market, in privately negotiated transactions or by other means.
  • The company said the repurchases will be funded from available cash and equivalents, working capital, credit facility capacity, or cash flows from operations.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: AgriFORCE to conduct reverse stock split
data.description:
• AgriFORCE Growing Systems (NASDAQ:AGRI) said it will conduct a reverse stock split.
  • In the split every one 100 shares will automatically be combined into one share.
  • The split is being implemented for the purpose of regaining compliance with the minimum bid price requirement.
